pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh:8 and export_result.sh:60; consistent across all task scripts
Web search confirmed as official U.S. Department of the Treasury website (home.treasury.gov)
Web search confirmed as official Social Security Administration website
Web search confirmed as official Securities and Exchange Commission website
Web search confirmed as official U.S. Government portal
Web search confirmed as official Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency website
Task description requires 'Certificate Issuer (CA)' - matches metadata requirement. Verifier checks for known CA names (line 81-82)
Task description requires 'Validity Period (start/end dates)' - matches metadata requirement. Verifier checks for date patterns (line 88)
Task description requires 'Public Key type/size (e.g., RSA 2048)' - matches metadata requirement. Verifier checks for RSA/ECDSA and bit lengths (line 85)
Task description requires 'TLS protocol version used' - matches metadata requirement. Verifier checks for TLS version patterns (line 91)
Verifier code line 77 checks 'if len(content) > 100:' which requires strictly greater than 100, not equal to or greater than 100

privileged_info_summary: The task requires auditing TLS certificates on five real U.S. government websites: treasury.gov (Department of Treasury), ssa.gov (Social Security Administration), sec.gov (Securities and Exchange Commission), usa.gov (U.S. Government portal), and cisa.gov (Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency). The audit report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/tls_audit_report.txt and must contain four types of information: Certificate Issuer/CA name, Validity period with start/end dates, Public Key type and size (e.g., RSA 2048), and TLS protocol version (1.2 or 1.3). The report must contain more than 100 characters of content to pass verification. One discrepancy found: metadata claims minimum report size of 100 bytes, but verifier requires strictly greater than 100.
